observations: Clean GGUFConverter class handles model conversion from HuggingFace to GGUF format
Well-designed search for pre-converted GGUF models on HuggingFace Hub
Proper error handling for missing dependencies and model loading failures
Comprehensive health check with detailed backend status information
Backend initialization tests with mocked llama-cpp-python
Health check tests verify all status fields
Generate and chat method tests for both sync and streaming modes
Error handling tests for uninitialized backend
GGUFConverter tests for finding pre-converted models
Proper separation between LlamaCppBackend and LlamaCppBackendStub
Support for both direct GGUF paths and HuggingFace model conversion

nonBlocking.description: convert_hf_to_gguf method relies on external convert_hf_to_gguf.py script which may not be available
nonBlocking.suggestion: Consider adding fallback to ctransformers or transformers' built-in GGUF export if available

nonBlocking.description: GGUFConverter.find_preconverted_gguf has hardcoded model variants that may not cover all cases
nonBlocking.suggestion: Make GGUF variant search patterns configurable

sharedStateObservations.observation: llama.cpp backend handles missing llama-cpp-python gracefully with stub implementation. Pattern: check LLAMA_CPP_AVAILABLE constant before importing.
GGUF model loading supports both direct file paths and HuggingFace model IDs. For HF models, searches for pre-converted GGUF variants from TheBloke and others.
llama.cpp supports CPU inference (n_gpu_layers=0) and GPU offloading (n_gpu_layers > 0). Metal support on macOS, CUDA on Linux/Windows.
